snippet: Medical Intervention Support (MIS) funding supports schools to engage appropriately trained staff to assist students who require regular, complex medical support at school. MIS funding is available to support schools to put in place the necessary work arrangements for the delivery of specialised assistance to these students. This involves the engagement of appropriately trained Education Support (ES) staff to undertake duties and responsibilities for delivering supporting procedures, consistent with Level 1, Range 2 of the ES Class. The ES staff members must have undergone specific training to support the students' medical needs. Training may be provided by an appropriate healthcare provider, or through the Schoolcare Program External Link . Schools with the following campus types are eligible for Medical Intervention Support funding at campus level: Funding is calculated for each of the four school terms and is allocated through credit funding.
